jobs.description:
This role offers the opportunity to work on complex technical challenges and develop innovative solutions in a collaborative engineering environment.

Within the Transportation Technology and Services organization, Relay Central provides a seamless, intelligent, and trusted foundation that supports Amazon’s middle-mile carriers in operating effectively within a dynamic and evolving ecosystem. Our products are designed to enable carriers to focus on safety, reliability, and operational performance while supporting business growth with Amazon.

By bringing essential capabilities together into a cohesive experience, Relay Central reduces operational friction, supports informed decision-making, and enables carriers to operate with confidence. Our vision is to serve as the backbone of Amazon Relay through thoughtful design, strong system integrations, and continuous improvement. We work to simplify complexity, present relevant insights when they are needed, and scale to meet the changing needs of the transportation network. Relay Central aims to be a trusted operational platform that supports carrier success and helps shape the future of transportation.

Relay Central delivers various programs, while maintaining high standards for safety, compliance, trust, and delivery quality.

The team is seeking individuals who are interested in contributing to the transformation of transportation and logistics operations through technology and collaboration.


Key job responsibilities
We’re looking for strong software leaders who are passionate about building delightful, high-impact shopping experiences for customers. This is a multidisciplinary role focused on work across recommendation and personalization systems.

* You will be responsible for leading the team by setting the team's technical vision, architecture, and development practices.
* You will be responsible for delivering key customer facing capabilities, delivering high quality artifacts.
* You will be responsible for raising the bar for the team's culture and helping grow the talent on the team.
* You will work closely across product, science, and design job families to come up with new solutions for customers.

- Knowledge of distributed systems design and implementation or equivalent
jobs.description:
U Emerging Markets — Belgium, Netherlands, Poland, Sweden, and Ireland — represent over a quarter of Europe's population and nearly a quarter of EU5 GDP, yet remain one of Amazon's largest untapped growth opportunities on the continent. The E3 (Emerging, Exports & Expansions) Tech organization exists to close that gap.

As a Sr. SDE on the E3 Emerging Markets team, you will design and own the distributed systems and services that directly shape how customers discover products, how sellers expand across borders, and how Amazon competes with entrenched local players across five high-growth European markets. This is a greenfield-heavy, high-ownership role — you will architect next-generation capabilities in local selection, same-day delivery CX, and automated seller expansion, working from our Bangalore engineering hub alongside teams in Luxembourg and Hyderabad. Every system you build has a direct line to customer experience and the P&L.

Key job responsibilities

Design, build, and own distributed systems and services that power customer experience, seller experience, and fulfillment capabilities across EU Emerging Markets stores
Lead technical design and architecture for high-impact, time-sensitive initiatives — including same-day delivery CX features (FST, storefront, basket-building), AI/ML-powered local selection identification, and automated seller expansion platforms
Work across ambiguous, greenfield problem spaces where requirements evolve and the right solution requires both technical depth and business judgment
Partner with EU5 host teams, product managers, and business stakeholders to secure prioritization for EM-specific needs and deliver incremental launches without sacrificing architectural integrity
Drive data-driven decision-making — instrument systems for metric monitoring, build automation for auditing, and own the business outcomes your systems enable
Mentor engineers, conduct rigorous code reviews, and raise the technical bar of the team
Contribute to the E3 GenAI productivity strategy — leveraging Amazon Config Service (ACS), Freebird, and Amazon GenAI tools to drive meaningful efficiency gains in feature delivery

A day in the life
You might start the morning reviewing the technical design for a new local brand identification model — an AI/ML solution that needs to reliably surface locally relevant selection across five EU markets at scale. By afternoon, you're in a design review for the Same-Day CX feature set launching across NL/BE/SE/IE, working through the FST and storefront defaulting logic with engineers in Luxembourg. Later, you're unblocking a junior engineer on the PanEU FBA Demand implementation, and closing the day by reviewing metrics from the Belgium MFC launch pipeline. Every decision you make has a direct line to customer experience and the P&L.

About the team
Amazon's EU Emerging Markets stores — Belgium, Netherlands, Poland, Sweden, and Ireland — represent 26% of Europe's population and 24% of EU5 GDP, yet remain one of Amazon's largest untapped growth opportunities on the continent. That gap is the opportunity.

The E3 Tech organization was established to close it. We are a newly unified engineering team spanning Emerging Markets, Exports, and Expansion, operating under a single technical charter to build the systems that will make Amazon the preferred shopping destination across Europe's fastest-growing markets. We work on greenfield problems with real P&L stakes — every system we build directly impacts customer experience, seller success, and Amazon's path to profitability in these stores.

We are building our Bangalore engineering team as a core part of this mission. You will work alongside engineers in Luxembourg and Hyderabad, partnering closely with EU business, product, and partner teams to deliver at Amazon scale.

jobs.description:
We are the Last Mile Delivery Design team for Amazon Worldwide Operations. We define, build, and maintain the products that Amazon businesses use to deliver packages to customers around the world. Our team of experienced designers, researchers, and cartographers partners with product teams to understand user behaviors and needs, creating high-quality experiences that strive to not only meet those needs, but exceed them. We design experiences such as the Amazon mobile delivery platform, in-vehicle digital tools, DSP 2.0, Delivery Station tools, and other systems that support addresses, geolocation, routing, and deliveries. The experiences we create are used by multiple Amazon businesses and product lines, including Amazon Logistics, Prime Now, Amazon Fresh, Amazon Flex, Amazon Key, Lockers, I Have Space, Retail, and more.

Key job responsibilities
Our team is looking for high-performing design leaders to drive both big-picture vision and fast-paced execution, delivering near-term results while exploring future opportunities. This Senior UX Design role focuses on helping transporters safely and effectively deliver packages for our customers, providing clarity on where they they need to go, what they need to do, and the best way to get there. Success requires close collaboration with cartographers, engineers, and product managers. Many of our projects extend beyond digital interfaces, connecting with real-world physical processes. We work hands-on in our design and prototyping studio, deeply understanding customer needs and creating experiences that aim to meet them in the moments that matter most.

A day in the life
As a designer on the Last Mile Experience Design team, you will own your work end-to-end, from managing your UX backlog to developing, testing, and sharing design strategies. You’ll tackle complex design challenges with creative solutions and collaborate closely with product leaders, cartographers, and engineering teams to create robust experiences that help drivers navigate their routes safely, clearly, and efficiently. Strong collaboration and facilitation skills are essential to bring clarity and drive alignment across teams. You’ll be expected to uphold high design standards, continuously improve how we work, and advocate for quality user experiences. With customer needs at the forefront, you’ll use sound judgment, stay informed about our users, competitors, and industry trends, and contribute to delivering best-in-class navigation solutions.

About the team
We lead with vision and follow a flexible design process, adapting our approach to fit the needs of the moment. We invent through collaborative brainstorming, prototype and simulate experiences with real customers, and share our vision across teams and leadership. We scale empathy for our users to partners and leaders, ensuring our tech teams have what they need to build great products. Equally important, we prioritize a healthy work-life balance. While we are a team of high achievers, we recognize that sustainable performance requires realistic scoping and mindful workload management. We know innovation suffers when designers are stretched too thin or working unsustainable hours (many of us have families, too). Though we may put in extra effort to polish a critical project, we strive to minimize thrash, accurately scope work, and maintain a healthy, balanced work week for every person on the team.

jobs.description:
People Experience and Technology Excellenceチームでは、人事領域においてテクノロジーを活用しイノベーションをおこせるHRアソシエイトパートナーを募集しています。

私たちはHRビジネスパートナー(HRBP)と緊密に連携し、全国の物流拠点やコーポレートオフィスで勤務する社員の皆様をカスタマーとして、人事業務の遂行およびプロセス改善を推進していきます。
今回は日本国内や海外のステークホルダーと協働して、コーポレート部門や新興ビジネス部門を担当いただきます。

HRアソシエイトパートナーはサポート業務ではありません。担当拠点や部門のパートナーとして、Amazonの文化を維持しながら組織を強化し、従業員が互いに学び、成長できる就業環境を構築する重要な役割です。 ぜひ、私達と共にAmazonの新たな歴史をつくりましょう！

Key job responsibilities
<業務内容> HRBPと連携し、人事部の顔として以下の人事業務を責任をもって遂行いただきます。
・人事部門のフロントライン業務（主に社員からの問合せの一次対応）
・社員からの問い合わせを受ける事を通じて、社員エクスペリエンスの向上
・セルフサービスの推進によりストレスフリーな社員エクスペリエンスの提供
・入社オリエンテーションの実施や手続き業務
・人事データ管理業務・人事手続き業務（人事異動、出張に伴う手続き、勤怠管理など）
・社員の健康診断、予防接種、ストレスチェックの準備や管理業務
・安全衛生委員会の資料作成や産業医との折衝
・社内のエンゲージメントイベントやミーティングのコーディネーション業務
・人事関連や人事業務改善プロジェクトへの参画
・拠点立ち上げにかかる人事業務プロセスの整備、運用、改善

A day in the life
<会社のカルチャー>
Amazonでは全員がリーダーです。個人の裁量に任される部分が多く、社員個々人の可能性を最大限に伸ばす事を重視しています。様々なバックグラウンドを持った社員が前職で培った多様な経験やスキルを活かして職位や役割に関わらず誰もがチャレンジできる環境です。

Amazon は多様かつインクルーシブな職場づくりを目指しています。Amazonは男女雇用機会均等法を順守しています。人種、 出身国、性別、性的指向、障がい、年齢、その他の属性によって差別することなく、平等に採用選考の機会を提供しています。
障がいをお持ちの方は、以下をご覧ください。https://www.amazon.jobs/disability/jp

About the team
私たちPXT Excellenceは拡大するビジネスニーズに対応するため、複数部門に所属していたHRアソシエイトパートナーを集約化した組織として2024年6月に立ち上がりました。私たちのビジョンは標準化された業務と革新的な解決方法により、ビジネスに最高クラスのパートナーシップを提供することを掲げており、これらを少数精鋭で達成していきます。

<キャリアパス>
HRアソシエイトパートナーからキャリアをスタートし、HRBPへキャリアップしているメンバーも多数在籍しています。アマゾンでは社内公募制度があり、国内外問わず他部署や異なる職種への異動にチャレンジし、社内で多様なキャリアを築くチャンスもあります。アマゾンではご自身で学び成長することを推奨し、キャリア開発できるようサポートを行っています。

<会社のカルチャー>
アマゾンでは全員がリーダーです。個人の裁量に任される部分が多く、社員の個々の可能性を最大限に伸ばす事を重視しています。様々なバックグラウンドを持った社員が前職で培った多様な経験やスキルを活かして職位や役割に関わらず誰もがチャレンジできる環境です。

Amazon は多様かつインクルーシブな職場づくりを目指しています。Amazonは男女雇用機会均等法を順守しています。人種、 出身国、性別、性的指向、障がい、年齢、その他の属性によって差別することなく、平等に採用選考の機会を提供しています。障がいをお持ちの方は、こちら をご覧ください。
　
<その他>
本ポジションの所属部門等に関する詳細は、下記リンク先をご参照ください。
・職種の紹介：人事
・部門の紹介：人事

- Experience in influencing senior leadership through data driven insights
- Experience effectively communicating complex concepts through written and verbal communication

jobs.description:
Application deadline: Mar 12, 2026

As the the Manager, Network Intelligence & Strategy, you will build and lead the network intelligence capability that underpins Amazon Health’s expanding provider ecosystem. This is a highly strategic role responsible for defining how Amazon evaluates markets, designs provider networks, and translates network insights into scalable partnerships across payers, employers, and health systems. This role operates at the intersection of strategy, analytics, and execution and will directly influence how Amazon scales its provider platform nationally.

You will define and scale the frameworks Amazon uses to prioritize markets, evaluate partnerships, and design high-performing provider networks. This includes building durable mechanisms that integrate access, economics, quality, and competitive dynamics into repeatable decision systems. You will establish the long-term architecture for how Amazon approaches network design in an increasingly hybrid model spanning owned assets, third-party partnerships, and virtual care. You will also serve as the primary analytical partner to the Chief Network Officer, helping shape Amazon’s transition toward more health plan–driven growth models.

This position plays a key role in shaping how Amazon evaluates long-term payer strategy and the evolving economics of national network platforms.

Key job responsibilities
Network Development and Partnerships
- Support national network expansion across health systems, specialty providers, and third party partners
- Identify, evaluate, and help structure new partnership opportunities aligned to One Medical’s growth strategy
- Manage a portfolio of strategic relationships spanning exploratory partnerships through scaled execution
- Represent Amazon One Medical in external meetings with senior executives and partners
- Shape partnership constructs including clinical integration models, data collaboration, and market expansion approaches
Chief Network Officer Support
- Serve as a strategic partner to the Chief Network Officer on priority initiatives and leadership agendas
- Drive preparation for executive forums including MBRs, WBRs, and strategic reviews
- Translate leadership direction into actionable workstreams and track execution across stakeholders
Program Leadership and Execution
- Coordinate across clinical, payer, enterprise, and operations teams to drive alignment and execution
- Establish mechanisms to track progress, surface risks, and ensure delivery against milestones
- Bring structure to ambiguous problem spaces and drive outcomes through influence rather than authority
Internal and External Stakeholder Management
- Act as a trusted interface between the network team and broader Amazon Health Services leadership
- Build strong working relationships across Commercial, Seniors, Enterprise, and Product teams
- Support partner governance including executive touchpoints, performance tracking, and escalation management

jobs.description:
Join our dynamic team and become a critical guardian of technological infrastructure! As a Data Center Engineering Technician, you'll play a pivotal role in maintaining the heartbeat of mission-critical technology infrastructure, ensuring seamless operations that power global digital experiences. Your expertise will directly contribute to maintaining an extraordinary 99.999% uptime, keeping the digital world running smoothly and efficiently.

Engineering Technicians are Amazon’s front line when it comes to hands-on electrical and mechanical equipment troubleshooting. They will maintain, operate, and troubleshoot, Data Center Operation’s Mission Critical Facilities, which includes stand-by diesel generators and related fuel systems, as well as three phase electrical systems such as: switch gear, UPSs, PDUs, wet cell batteries and associated systems, CRAC, centrifugal chillers, cooling towers/water chemical system, air handlers and associated systems, pumps, & motors.

AWS Infrastructure Services owns the design, planning, delivery, and operation of all AWS global infrastructure. In other words, we’re the people who keep the cloud running. We support all AWS data centers and all of the servers, storage, networking, power, and cooling equipment that ensure our customers have continual access to the innovation they rely on. We work on the most challenging problems, with thousands of variables impacting the supply chain — and we’re looking for talented people who want to help.

You’ll join a diverse team of software, hardware, and network engineers, supply chain specialists, security experts, operations managers, and other vital roles. You’ll collaborate with people across AWS to help us deliver the highest standards for safety and security while providing seemingly infinite capacity at the lowest possible cost for our customers. And you’ll experience an inclusive culture that welcomes bold ideas and empowers you to own them to completion.

NOTE: Amazon does not sponsor for immigration, including for H-1B, TN, and other non-immigrant visas, for this role.

NOTE: Lump sum stipend will be provided to eligible candidates who relocate for this position.

10045

Key job responsibilities
- Daily building rounds to monitor and record information from electrical, mechanical, and fire/life safety equipment to ensure 24/7 reliability and availability
- Monitor Building Management Systems (BMS) and Electrical Power Management System (EPMS)
- Assist in troubleshooting of facility and rack-level events within internal Service Level Agreements (SLA)
- Perform rack installs and rack decommissioning within internal SLA
- Perform PM, troubleshooting, root cause analysis, and repair of electrical and mechanical equipment
- Technical writing to support change management program
- Monitor daily work requests and manage to resolution, and execute projects from conception to completion
- On-site support of contractors, sub-contractors and vendors, ensuring that all work performed is in accordance with established practices, procedures & local legislation
- Work schedule changes depending on specific site needs. Shifts can be up to 12-hours and may rotate on a predefined schedule. Some locations have on-call rotations.
- Role may support more than one location/site.

Physical Requirements:
Willingness and demonstrated ability to work in the following physical conditions with reasonable accommodations:
- Regularly lift and/or move up to 39 pounds independently
- Participate in group lifts for 40+ pounds
- Maintain balance and perform construction tasks while on a ladder
- Regularly walk, use hands and fingers, handle or feel, reach with hands and arms, stoop, kneel, crouch or crawl, climb stairs, twist, bend, work above shoulders, grasp, carry, push and pull objects into position
- Work in an environment requiring provided ear protection

This role supports data centers in various operational stages. The position may be assigned to a facility still under development, which could require travel to established locations for training until your designated site becomes fully operational.

Essential Requirements:

- Ability to travel to or commute between data center locations as needed

- Willingness to temporarily work at alternative sites during training periods or until assigned facility is operational

- Travel frequency will vary based on business needs and operational status of assigned facility

Candidates must be able to travel (over 50 miles) or commute (less than 50 miles) to active data center sites as required by business needs.

A day in the life
This role acts as Amazon’s front line when it comes to hands-on electrical and mechanical equipment preventative maintenance (PM) and troubleshooting, reporting to a site’s Data Center Facility Manager (FM). They will maintain, operate, and troubleshoot mission-critical data center facility equipment including stand-by diesel generators and related fuel systems, 3 phase electrical systems such as switchgear, UPS units, PDUs, and wet cell batteries and associated systems. Mechanical equipment includes CRAHU units, centrifugal chillers, cooling towers, water systems, air handlers and associated systems, pumps, and motors. Additional support equipment is included in the scope of the role which includes fire/life safety equipment, building automation systems, and general facilities equipment.

About the team
Diverse Experiences AWS values diverse experiences. Even if you do not meet all of the preferred qualifications and skills listed in the job description, we encourage candidates to apply. If your career is just starting, hasn’t followed a traditional path, or includes alternative experiences, don’t let it stop you from applying.

Why AWS? Amazon Web Services (AWS) is the world’s most comprehensive and broadly adopted cloud platform. We pioneered cloud computing and never stopped innovating — that’s why customers from the most successful startups to Global 500 companies trust our robust suite of products and services to power their businesses.

Inclusive Team Culture
AWS values curiosity and connection. Our employee-led and company-sponsored affinity groups promote inclusion and empower our people to take pride in what makes us unique. Our inclusion of events fosters stronger, more collaborative teams. Our continual innovation is fueled by the bold ideas, fresh perspectives, and passionate voices our teams bring to everything we do.

Mentorship & Career Growth
We’re continuously raising our performance bar as we strive to become Earth’s Best Employer. That’s why you’ll find endless knowledge-sharing, mentorship, and other career-advancing resources here to help you develop into a better-rounded professional.

Work/Life Balance
We value work-life harmony. Achieving success at work should never come at the expense of sacrifices at home, which is why we strive for flexibility as part of our working culture. When we feel supported in the workplace and at home, there’s nothing we can’t achieve.

jobs.basic_qualifications:
- 10+ years of professional non-internship marketing experience
- Bachelor's degree in marketing, communications, business, or equivalent
jobs.description:
As a senior leader within Amazon's University Talent Acquisition (AUTA) organization, the Sr. Manager of University Marketing, Outreach, & Engagement will drive Amazon's global school engagement and marketing strategy across hundreds of universities worldwide. This role is pivotal in shaping and elevating Amazon's employer brand presence on campuses, positioning the company as the employer of choice for top student talent while building strategic partnerships that influence future talent pipelines.

In this capacity, the senior leader will directly lead a team of school outreach and marketing professionals while indirectly leading University Relations Leads (URLs) who manage key relationships with universities. The role includes overseeing the strategic direction and successful management of campus brand ambassadors and executive school captain programs. The leader will foster a culture of innovation and strategic thinking within the team and collaborate cross-functionally with program teams, recruiting, experience, and digital marketing teams to ensure alignment and impact across the campus ecosystem. The leader will build messaging frameworks and collaborate with PR and legal teams for crisis communications and general communications, ensuring recruiters are prepared to communicate swiftly, clearly, and effectively.

The senior leader will develop and lead a comprehensive recruitment marketing strategy that drives inquiry generation, application growth, and contributes to hiring outcomes in alignment with the Amazon’s brand on university campuses across the world. Leveraging market research, demographic analysis, and digital engagement data, the Sr. Manager will identify growth opportunities and refine recruitment marketing strategies and experiences to maximize impact. A critical component of this role involves supporting recruiters in adopting and effectively translating data-driven insights into their daily engagement with prospective students, ensuring seamless integration between marketing strategy and front line recruitment execution. The ideal candidate is a strategic thinker with a passion for culture, an innovative mindset, and the ability to translate complex ideas into compelling narratives and experiences that attract top student talent across the globe.

Key job responsibilities
Brand Strategy & Marketing • Drive university outreach and marketing strategy aligned to Amazon's student talent positioning, ensuring brand consistency across all campus engagement channels • Leverage data-driven insights to resonate with Generation Z and Alpha talent segments • Position Amazon as employer of choice in emerging technology fields including Generative AI and robotics • Monitor employer brand trends, competitive landscape, and talent market shifts

Strategic Partnerships & Engagement • Build strategic partnerships with university leadership, faculty, and career services to influence curriculum and identify top talent • Collaborate with senior leaders on academic partnerships and campus initiatives • Develop data-sharing university partnership models to optimize engagement effectiveness

Program Development & Execution • Oversee innovative campus programs including technical events, case competitions, and executive speaking engagements • Design programs delivering meaningful experiences that drive talent attraction, engagement, and retention • Align campus initiatives with business objectives and measurable results

Measurement & Marketing Optimization • Develop metrics and analytics frameworks measuring school engagement effectiveness • • Optimize programs based on data-driven insights and ROI • Allocate resources to maximize marketing campaign ROI


About the team
At Amazon University Talent Acquisition (AUTA), we're revolutionizing how the world's most innovative company attracts and develops its next generation of leaders. As Amazon's early career pipeline, we manage global university recruiting across internships, full-time roles, and apprenticeships through cutting-edge AI technology and strategic partnerships with top universities worldwide. Our team operates at massive scale while maintaining an unwavering focus on finding the best talent, driving innovation in candidate experience through personalized journeys, and identifying emerging talent in fields like Generative AI and Robotics that will shape the world's future.

jobs.description:
Elevate Financial Strategy at the Forefront of Global Entertainment

We're seeking a strategic financial leader who will transform data into insights that drive Amazon Prime Video's international growth. In this pivotal role, you'll craft compelling financial narratives that shape critical business decisions and illuminate the path forward for our global entertainment platform.

Key job responsibilities

- Serve as the primary financial partner for Prime Video International, delivering comprehensive financial insights and support
- Develop sophisticated, data-driven financial analyses that illuminate business performance and strategic opportunities
- Consolidate and articulate financial performance across diverse international teams
- Collaborate with cross-functional teams to identify process improvements and technological innovations
- Lead comprehensive financial reviews and strategic initiatives with precision and creativity


A day in the life
Your day will be a dynamic blend of financial analysis, strategic collaboration, and innovative problem-solving. You'll dive deep into complex financial landscapes, translating intricate data into clear, actionable insights that empower our leadership team to make informed decisions.

About the team
We are a collaborative and forward-thinking finance team dedicated to supporting Prime Video's global expansion. Our work directly impacts how millions of customers experience entertainment worldwide, creating meaningful connections through storytelling and technology.

jobs.basic_qualifications:
- Bachelor's degree in Mechanical or Electrical Engineering or a related field
- 5+ years of work in a management position with 5 or more direct reports experience
- 5+ years of work in data centers with an emphasis on building and equipment operation experience
jobs.description:
AWS Infrastructure Services owns the design, planning, delivery, and operation of all AWS global infrastructure. In other words, we’re the people who keep the cloud running. We support all AWS data centers and all of the servers, storage, networking, power, and cooling equipment that ensure our customers have continual access to the innovation they rely on. We work on the most challenging problems, with thousands of variables impacting the supply chain — and we’re looking for talented people who want to help.

You’ll join a diverse team of software, hardware, and network engineers, supply chain specialists, security experts, operations managers, and other vital roles. You’ll collaborate with people across AWS to help us deliver the highest standards for safety and security while providing seemingly infinite capacity at the lowest possible cost for our customers. And you’ll experience an inclusive culture that welcomes bold ideas and empowers you to own them to completion.

We are seeking an Data Center Facility Manager to serve as a technical resource and leader within Amazon data centers. The position will help ensure overall availability and reliability to meet or exceed defined service levels of data center operations. The Data Center Facility Manager is responsible for the overall operation and maintenance of all electrical, mechanical, and HVAC equipment within the data center. The Data Center Facility Manager will assist in maintaining a high reliability and performance while keeping operating costs in facilities at a minimum. This equipment supports mission-critical servers and must maintain better than 99.999% uptime.

This role acts as Amazon’s front line when it comes to hands-on electrical and mechanical equipment troubleshooting. They will maintain, operate, and troubleshoot mission-critical data center facility equipment including electrical support equipment such as stand-by diesel generators and related fuel systems, 3 phase electrical systems that include but not limited to switchgear, UPS units, PDUs, and wet cell batteries and associated systems. Mechanical equipment includes CRAC units, centrifugal chillers, cooling towers/water chemical system, air handlers and associated systems, pumps, and motors. Additional support equipment is included in the scope of the role which includes fire suppression systems, building automation systems, and general facilities equipment.

Key job responsibilities
- Oversee all aspects of the data center's critical physical infrastructure. Ensure that all work performed within the space is done to high quality and without impact to internal/external customers
- Manage teams of 24x7 engineering technicians in all facets of their career
- Engage in improvement projects, often requiring reaching out to a variety of support teams, and drive them from conception to completion
- Coordinates daily with a multitude of third party vendors ensuring adherence to contracted SLAs
- Effectively and efficiently manage the operations budget and expenditures
- Routinely operate as the afterhours on-call Data Center Facility Manager for the data centers in the region. This will include responding to any issues within the data centers and managing the investigation, mitigation, and recovery of the issue(s)

A day in the life
As the Facility Manager, your role demonstrates a strong commitment to prioritizing the development and well-being of team members, as well as fostering diversity and inclusion. You will oversee all facets of the data center's critical infrastructure with a focus on continuous availability and optimal performance, while upholding high-quality standards and minimizing any impact on internal and external customers. Additionally, you will play a crucial role in process optimization, staff management, setting performance metrics, and driving continuous improvement initiatives, all while ensuring a supportive and inclusive work environment.

- Manage teams of 24x7 engineering technicians: Prioritize and assign tasks, coach and create documentation, guide and train staff on best practices.
- Effectively and efficiently manage operations budget: Monitor and control expenditures, ensure optimal resource allocation.
- Engage in improvement projects: Drive projects from conception to completion, collaborate with support teams.
- Coordinate with third-party vendors: Ensure adherence to contracted service level agreements (SLAs).
- Communicate complex technical information: Adapt communication style for diverse stakeholders, including non-technical audiences.
- Respond to out-of-hours emergency calls: Address urgent issues that may arise outside regular working hours.
- Act as an escalation point for facilities-related issues: Oversee routine and emergency services, ensure safety procedures are followed.
- Deliver operational excellence: Prioritize team development and well-being while achieving operational goals.
- Foster a diverse and inclusive team culture: Empower team members to reach their full potential.
- Perform physical tasks: Lift and move objects of varying weights, walk job sites in different terrains while maintaining balance and safety.
- Promote accessible work environments: Support employees' diverse needs.
- Work in noisy environments when required: Utilize approved personal protective equipment (PPE).
- Support multiple locations/sites: Coordinate and manage operations across different sites.
- Provide on-site management for builders, five days a week. (No remote work)

About the team
Amazon Web Services (AWS) is the world’s most comprehensive and broadly adopted cloud platform. We pioneered cloud computing and never stopped innovating — that’s why customers from the most successful startups to Global 500 companies trust our robust suite of products and services to power their businesses.

Amazon values diverse experiences. Even if you do not meet all of the preferred qualifications and skills listed in the job description, we encourage candidates to apply. If your career is just starting, hasn’t followed a traditional path, or includes alternative experiences, don’t let it stop you from applying.

We value work-life harmony. Achieving success at work should never come at the expense of sacrifices at home, which is why we strive for flexibility as part of our working culture. When we feel supported in the workplace and at home, there’s nothing we can’t achieve in the cloud.

Here at AWS, it’s in our nature to learn and be curious. Our employee-led affinity groups foster a culture of inclusion that empower us to be proud of our differences. Ongoing events and learning experiences, including our Conversations on Race and Ethnicity (CORE) and AmazeCon (gender diversity) conferences, inspire us to never stop embracing our uniqueness.

We’re continuously raising our performance bar as we strive to become Earth’s Best Employer. That’s why you’ll find endless knowledge-sharing, mentorship and other career-advancing resources here to help you develop into a better-rounded professional.

jobs.title: Data Center Facilities Manager

jobs.basic_qualifications:
- High school or equivalent diploma
- 1+ years of computer/server hardware troubleshooting or related IT experience
- Ability to work on a flexible schedule including days, nights, and/or weekends.
jobs.description:
Join our dynamic AWS team and become a critical guardian of global cloud infrastructure! You'll play a pivotal role in maintaining the heartbeat of the world's most innovative technology platform, ensuring seamless data center operations that power millions of businesses and services worldwide.

Data Center Technicians should be willing to work both independently and with a team. Work prioritization, organizational skills, effective communication, and the ability to react quickly are critical to success. In addition to hardware and network repair, candidates will install equipment, create documentation, innovate solutions, and fix problems within the data center space. This team works in an environment that operates 24/7. Traveling within and outside of the regional work area is required.

AWS Infrastructure Services owns the design, planning, delivery, and operation of all AWS global infrastructure. In other words, we’re the people who keep the cloud running. We support all AWS data centers and all of the servers, storage, networking, power, and cooling equipment that ensure our customers have continual access to the innovation they rely on. We work on the most challenging problems, with thousands of variables impacting the supply chain — and we’re looking for talented people who want to help.

You’ll join a diverse team of software, hardware, and network engineers, supply chain specialists, security experts, operations managers, and other vital roles. You’ll collaborate with people across AWS to help us deliver the highest standards for safety and security while providing seemingly infinite capacity at the lowest possible cost for our customers. And you’ll experience an inclusive culture that welcomes bold ideas and empowers you to own them to completion.
NOTE: Amazon does not sponsor for immigration, including for H-1B, TN, and other non-immigrant visas, for this role.
NOTE: Lump sum stipend will be provided to eligible candidates who relocate for this position.

Physical requirements:
• Regularly lift and/or move up to 40 pounds; and participate in group lifts for 41+ pounds
• Working in cramped and/or elevated locations
• Bending, lifting, stretching, and reaching
• Standing and walking for up to 8+ hours a day
• Ascending and descending ladders, stairs, and gangways safely and without limitation
• Work in a noisy environment
• Work shifts longer than 8 hours to support 24/7 operations (covering both night and day shifts).
This role supports data centers in various operational stages. The position may be assigned to a facility still under development, which could require travel to established locations for training until your designated site becomes fully operational.

Essential Requirements:
• Ability to travel to or commute between data center locations as needed
• Willingness to temporarily work at alternative sites during training periods or until assigned facility is operational
• Travel frequency will vary based on business needs and operational status of assigned facility
Candidates must be able to travel (over 50 miles) or commute (less than 50 miles) to active data center sites as required by business needs.



Key job responsibilities
• Performing server rack installations, hardware break-fix on various components, troubleshooting network issues, and responding to operational incidents that impact service availability, escalating to senior technicians and management as needed/required
• Serving as a primary contact point for both internal and external stakeholders, including engineers, software developers, vendors, and contractors
• Performing on-call duties and participating in scheduled maintenance and change management activities
• Contributing to documentation and process improvement initiatives based on your analysis of operational issues
• Helping to train and onboard new team members


A day in the life
As a Data Center Technician professional, you have industry-leading technical abilities and demonstrate a breadth of knowledge while you:
• Take ownership of technical issues brought by their customer base, engaging other teams when needed to drive resolution
• Solve problems at their root and step back to understand the broader context
• Maintain service level agreements through the implementation of proactive issue detection and reporting
You will be required to work shift work that will include days/nights/weekends/holidays. Traveling within and outside of the regional work area is required. You will be responsible for having a reliable personal vehicle and valid driver's license to travel within the regional work area, as company transportation will not be provided.
